inputs.prompt: Name/company: Elena Cruz, Waypoint Films. Project: After Hours — a documentary following night-shift workers in five different professions across one city, revealing a parallel world most people never see. Format: 4 x 52 min series. Recipient: Head of Factual at a major streaming platform. Stage: Treatment and director showreel ready. Ask: 20-minute introductory call.
Name: James Park. Project: podcast about forgotten inventors. Recipient: Commissioning editor at a podcast network. Ask: read my proposal.

Name/company: Maria Lindqvist, Nordisk Produktion. Project: The Ferry — a documentary series following the crew and passengers of a daily ferry between Estonia and Finland. Format: 6 x 30 min. Recipient: Head of Documentary Programming at YLE (Finland). Connection: Met briefly at Nordisk Forum last year. Stage: Development funded, sizzle reel complete. Ask: Formal pitch meeting.

Name/company: Amina Odhiambo, Savanna Stories. Project: The Matatu Diaries — a documentary podcast capturing conversations and stories from passengers riding matatu minibuses across Nairobi, exploring class, migration, and daily survival through overheard dialogue. Format: 12-episode podcast series, 25 min each. Recipient: Head of Content at a major East African digital media platform. Connection: No prior contact. Stage: Pilot episode recorded, three episodes scripted. Ask: 15-minute call to discuss distribution partnership.

inputs.description: Adversarial — recipient who dislikes cold emails
inputs.prompt: Name/company: David Chen, Ironside Productions. Project: Concrete Gardens — a documentary about guerrilla gardeners transforming abandoned urban lots into food forests without municipal permission, and the legal battles that follow. Format: Single film, 75 min. Recipient: A well-known documentary commissioning editor who has publicly stated on social media that they delete all unsolicited pitch emails and only take meetings through referrals. Connection: None. No mutual contacts identified. Stage: 40 minutes of footage shot, director's cut of a 10-minute proof-of-concept available. Ask: Permission to send a 2-minute sizzle reel.

inputs.description: East Asian context — Japanese business culture
inputs.prompt: Name/company: Kenji Tanaka, Shizuka Film Works. Project: The Last Craftsmen — a documentary series profiling artisans practising endangered traditional crafts in rural Japan: a swordsmith, a lacquerware maker, a tatami weaver, and a paper lantern painter, each with no apprentice to continue their work. Format: 4 x 45 min. Recipient: Senior commissioning producer at a Japanese public broadcaster's international co-production division. Connection: Introduced once at a reception during the Tokyo Docs forum but no substantive conversation. Stage: Detailed treatment in Japanese and English, 8 minutes of test footage, letters of access from three artisans. Ask: A formal meeting at the broadcaster's offices to present the project.

Name/company: Priya Menon, Tidal Wave Media (Mumbai). Project: The Lunch Network — a documentary about the 5,000-strong dabbawala lunch delivery network in Mumbai, exploring how an analogue logistics system with a near-zero error rate survives in a city obsessed with digital disruption. Format: Single film, 60 min. Recipient: Head of Factual Programming at an Indian streaming platform. Connection: No direct connection, but a mutual colleague at a production guild has offered to make an introduction if the recipient shows interest. Stage: Access confirmed with the dabbawala association, director has shot two days of observational test footage. Ask: A 20-minute video call to discuss commissioning interest.
